data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is designed for quality, focusing on movement efficiency and endurance. We’ll be going through 6 rounds of rowing or skiing, followed by some box step-ups, kettlebell sumo deadlift high pulls, and finishing off with ring rows. The goal here is to maintain good form and control throughout each movement while keeping a steady pace. Let’s break it down step by step!


---


Workout Components


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

Technique Steps:
  • **Rowing**: Sit on the rower with your feet secured in the straps. Start with your knees bent and arms extended.
  • **Catch Position**: Lean slightly forward, keeping your back straight.
  • **Drive**: Push through your legs, extend your hips, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close.
  • **Recovery**: Extend your arms first, then hinge at the hips and bend your knees to return to the catch position.

  • **Ski Erg**: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the handles.
  • **Start Position**: Slightly bend your knees and hinge at the hips.
  • **Pull**: Engage your core and pull the handles down towards your hips, using your arms and legs together.
  • **Return**: Let the handles rise back up while maintaining a slight bend in your knees.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your back straight and core engaged to avoid rounding your spine.
  • Use your legs to generate power, not just your arms.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • If you’re new to rowing or skiing, reduce the time to 2:00 or even 1:30.
  • Focus on technique rather than speed.

    • ---


    2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart.
  • Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box.
  • Bring your other foot up to meet the first on top of the box.
  • Step back down one foot at a time, maintaining control.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your chest up and engage your core throughout the movement.
  • Use a box height that allows you to maintain good form without excessive strain.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • Use a lower box if you’re new to step-ups or have mobility issues.
  • Hold onto a wall or a sturdy object for balance if needed.

    • ---


    3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out.
  • Place the kettlebell between your feet and hinge at your hips to grab the handle.
  • **Deadlift**: Push through your heels to lift the kettlebell, keeping it close to your body.
  • **High Pull**: As the kettlebell rises, pull your elbows high and wide, bringing it up to your chin level.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your back flat and chest up during the deadlift phase.
  • Ensure the kettlebell stays close to your body to maintain control.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • Use a lighter kettlebell if you’re struggling with form.
  • Perform the movement without the high pull (just a sumo deadlift) if needed.

    • ---


    4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back comfortably.
  • Grab the rings with an overhand grip and walk your feet forward until your body is at an angle.
  • Pull your chest towards the rings, keeping your elbows close to your body.
  • Lower yourself back down with control.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips.
  • Engage your core throughout the movement.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder.
  • If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a barbell or a TRX.

    • ---


    Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat**:
  • Row/Ski: 2:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 16/8” box
  • Kettlebell: 12kg/8kg
  • Ring Rows: 8 reps

  • **Train**:
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 20 reps
  • Kettlebell: 16kg/12kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

  • **Compete**:
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 24/16” box
  • Kettlebell: 24kg/16kg
  • Ring Rows: 12 reps

    • Strategy Advice:
  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the rounds. It’s better to break up the reps into manageable sets than to go all out and burn out early.
  • Prioritize form over speed; quality movements will yield better results in the long run.

    • ---


    Alright team, let’s get warmed up and ready to crush this WOD! Remember, it’s all about quality today, so let’s keep our movements sharp and our spirits high! 💪

Start with your knees bent and arms extended. - **Catch Position**: Lean slightly forward, keeping your back straight. - **Drive**: Push through your legs, extend your hips, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close. - **Recovery**: Extend your arms first, then hinge at the hips and bend your knees to return to the catch position. - **Ski Erg**: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the handles. - **Start Position**: Slightly bend your knees and hinge at the hips. - **Pull**: Engage your core and pull the handles down towards your hips, using your arms and legs together. - **Return**: Let the handles rise back up while maintaining a slight bend in your knees. #### Form Tips: - Keep your back straight and core engaged to avoid rounding your spine. - Use your legs to generate power, not just your arms. #### Scaling/Modifications: - If you’re new to rowing or skiing, reduce the time to 2:00 or even 1:30. - Focus on technique rather than speed. --- ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) #### Technique Steps: - Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart. - Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box. - Bring your other foot up to meet the first on top of the box. - Step back down one foot at a time, maintaining control. #### Form Tips: - Keep your chest up and engage your core throughout the movement. - Use a box height that allows you to maintain good form without excessive strain. #### Scaling/Modifications: - Use a lower box if you’re new to step-ups or have mobility issues. - Hold onto a wall or a sturdy object for balance if needed. --- ### 3. Ring Rows (10 reps) #### Technique Steps: - Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back comfortably. - Grab the rings with an overhand grip and walk your feet forward until your body is at an angle. - Pull your chest towards the rings, keeping your elbows close to your body. - Lower yourself back down with control. #### Form Tips: - Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips. - Engage your core throughout the movement. #### Scaling/Modifications: - Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder. - If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a barbell or a TRX. --- ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat**: - Row/Ski: 2:00 - Box Step-Ups: 16/8” box - Kettlebell: 12kg/8kg - Ring Rows: 8 reps - **Train**: - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 20 reps - Kettlebell: 16kg/12kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps - **Compete**: - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 24/16” box - Kettlebell: 24kg/16kg - Ring Rows: 12 reps ### Strategy Advice: - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the rounds. It’s better to break up the reps into manageable sets than to go all out and burn out early. - Prioritize form over speed; quality movements will yield better results in the long run. --- Alright team, let’s get warmed up and ready to crush this WOD! Remember, it’s all about quality today, so let’s keep our movements sharp and our spirits high! 💪
